44.reason: [The program reads only n_V (number of vertices) here but never reads the number of edges m. According to input format, it should read "n m" on the first line.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.125 , reason:  [The program reads only n_V (number of vertices) here but never reads the number of edges m. According to input format, it should read "n m" on the first line.]

53.reason: [This loop condition assumes that every adjacency list ends with a sentinel node (-1), but this is not standard and may cause segmentation fault when G[i].link is NULL. Also, the deletion logic is flawed and does not correctly remove all occurrences of 'num' from adjacency lists.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.125 , reason:  [This loop condition assumes that every adjacency list ends with a sentinel node (-1), but this is not standard and may cause segmentation fault when G[i].link is NULL. Also, the deletion logic is flawed and does not correctly remove all occurrences of 'num' from adjacency lists.]

94.reason: [This is incorrect: when inserting before head, e->next should be head, not head->next. This breaks the list.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.125 , reason:  [This is incorrect: when inserting before head, e->next should be head, not head->next. This breaks the list.]

118.reason: [This reads the number of edges, but the first input line was supposed to contain both n_V and n_W (m). Since n_V was already read in main(), this leads to misaligned input parsing.]

125.reason: [This inserts a sentinel -1 at index n_V, which is out of valid vertex range (0 to n_V-1). This causes undefined behavior and corrupts memory.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.125 , reason:  [This inserts a sentinel -1 at index n_V, which is out of valid vertex range (0 to n_V-1). This causes undefined behavior and corrupts memory.]

142.reason: [This condition skips printing isolated vertices (which shouldn't happen per problem constraints, but more critically, after deletion, vertex 0 must always be printed first. However, if vertex 0's adjacency list becomes empty due to bad deletion, it might not print, violating traversal rules.]

162.reason: [Same issue as in DFS: if a vertex has no neighbors (e.g., after faulty deletion), it won't be printed, breaking traversal output. Also, BFS should start from 0 and traverse connected component, but this prints v before enqueuing neighbors, yet the queue handling is inconsistent.]

76.reason: [Missing return value in case of empty queue; function should always return an int. This may cause undefined behavior.]
